"For he is our God; we are the people of his pasture, the sheep he owns. Today, if only you would obey him!"
‭‭Psalms‬ ‭95:7‬ ‭NET‬‬‬‬‬‬

The verses following this one tell how the people of Israel were obstinate in not trusting God to take care of them. When you think of God, what first comes to mind? Love? Grace? Mercy? Or, does God's judgment pop up first? Certainly, God has all of these attributes and more, to perfection. But when it comes to you, do you really trust God to do what is best for you?

"For this is the way God loved the world: He gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life."
‭‭John‬ ‭3:16‬ ‭NET‬‬‬‬‬‬

-Sean Lennon
Interview Text:
Debra:

(part 4)

She said she is an intercessor. She loves getting hours to pray for one person because as she prays for them new images open up and she prays them over the person. I asked her what it feels like to receive these images. How does she know they are the Holy Spirit. 

She thought and then said, "It's like the difference between a church hug, and a real hug. You know how when you see someone in the halls of the church you give them a quick hug?" She leaned toward me and gestured giving my a quick side-hug. "Well, now imagine one of those hugs that lasts a full minute when something really tragic has happened and you can just feel in the hug that the person really deeply cares for you. And there is a connection you can just feel. It's different. That's what it feels like when God speaks to me, she said. It just feels deeper and different than other stuff. It's like a river, she said, but then stopped. "No, it's not like a river because I don't want to make it sound like it feels wet." 

I asked, "Do you mean it is it like a current? Or like wind?" 

She nodded as if I was almost right. She gestured vaguely to her chest. 

I asked, "Is it focused in that area? Like in your chest." 

"Yes," she said. "And it spreads throughout your body. But, I was talking about what the presence of God feels like and what it feels like for the Holy Spirit to flow through me. As far as the visions go, I know they are from God because I can always locate them outside of my head. For example, I had a vision of a fairy angel. She was hovering above my left shoulder. When I have a vision of Jesus on the cross, I can literally reach out and touch the place where the vision is located. I had a vision of someone laying on my bed. This person needed emotional CPR so I literally did the motions of CPR on him. None of this happens inside my head.  

She said the main characteristic about God that always comes through is His goodness. His goodness and his amazing love.
Music Video Instructions: If you're hurting, and you feel alone, hear God telling you to hold on; you are not alone.
